bugOrUsabilitySummary: The selected setting detail pane was visible after the layout fix, but it presented a long flat list of raw/internal labels before user-facing meaning. Normal users had to interpret read/write metadata, pending validation labels, conflicts, and high-risk gates from debug-oriented wording.

rowsManuallyReviewed.expectedDetail: standard appearance row with clear current value, duplicate-conflict explanation when applicable, and edit controls
gated high-risk cursor/input row with runtime monitor-name oracle wording and no freeform-string implication
debug/crash high-risk row with strong crash-sensitive warning and gate wording
screen-shader row with screen-shader production gate and advisory wording preserved
display/render high-risk row with production high-risk gate and recovery warning
normal writable-row comparison with reviewed config-write explanation

detailPaneBefore.structure: single flat list of detail lines followed by write controls and safety notes
detailPaneBefore.problem: raw fields such as row id, read support, write support, comparison detail, write candidate status, pending review labels, source line, and duplicate line numbers appeared at the same level as user-facing content

detailPaneAfter.result: display name, official setting, category, current value status, editability, and safety warnings appear before raw metadata

advancedMetadataBehavior: raw/internal metadata moved into collapsed Source / advanced metadata expander
highRiskWarningBehavior: high-risk rows show user-facing warnings from high-risk write policy before advanced metadata; cursor.default_monitor adds monitor-name oracle wording; debug.manual_crash adds crash-sensitive wording; screen-shader gate wording is preserved
writeButtonExplanationBehavior: Apply state text is derived from pending validation and review state: conflicts, unreadable config, invalid values, high-risk gate requirements, and non-editable rows are explained in user-facing language
manualVerificationSteps: Select Appearance Blur Enabled and confirm the Setting, Current value, Edit, Safety, and Source / advanced metadata sections are visible.
If duplicate config entries are present for Appearance Blur Enabled, confirm Apply is disabled and the duplicate-resolution message is shown.
Search for cursor.default_monitor and confirm monitor-name oracle and high-risk cursor/input wording is visible.
Search for debug.manual_crash and confirm crash-sensitive gate wording is visible.
Search for decoration.screen_shader and confirm screen-shader advisory/gate wording is preserved.
Search for render.direct_scanout and confirm display/render high-risk wording is visible.
Search for windows.snap.enabled and confirm the normal reviewed config-write flow remains understandable.
Expand Source / advanced metadata and confirm raw metadata remains available.
proofUsed: existing RowDetailProjection fields for current value, pending validation, high-risk policy, and screen-shader advisory metadata
proofStillMissing: human visual confirmation on the user's actual display
nextRecommendedSprint: Manual UI visual pass and copy polish sprint
